title: GetResponse<T> and SetResponse<T=Value> generic response types with default type param are correct
description: GetResponse<T> and SetResponse<T = serde_json::Value> are generic over the object type rather than having per-method concrete response types. A future contributor might add per-method aliases (type ChatGetResponse = GetResponse<Chat>) or flatten these into concrete types. The generic approach is correct because RFC 8620 §5.1/§5.3 define the response envelope shape uniformly across all object types — the only variation is the element type. The default type parameter on SetResponse is load-bearing: message_create and read_position_set use SetResponse without a type argument, which avoids requiring callers to name a created-object type they do not need. Removing the default would force callers to write SetResponse<serde_json::Value> everywhere for update-only operations, which is noise. The #[serde(bound)] attribute on SetResponse is required because Rust's default derived bound is too conservative for generic structs with Option fields.
